fields.description: In charge of whole Financial Activities

fields.title: [Demo] Payment & subscription reminder
fields.message:
Dear station operator,

This is a sample payment-related broadcast, similar to a subscription or hosting invoice reminder.

Please ensure any platform fees are settled by the due date to avoid interruption. Contact your account manager if you need a copy of the invoice.

— FSERP (sample message)

fields.title: [Demo] Scheduled maintenance window
fields.message:
Maintenance notice (sample)

We may schedule brief maintenance on Sunday nights (example: 02:00–04:00 local time). During this window you could see short delays on sync or login.

We will confirm real windows through your production administrator.

— FSERP Platform

fields.title: [Demo] Welcome — Master Filling Station demo tenant
fields.message:
Welcome to the Master Filling Station demo tenant.

This company is pre-loaded with sample chart of accounts, nozzles, customers, and demo journals so you can try Cashier, Reports, and Payments without entering everything from scratch.

You can dismiss broadcasts from your dashboard after reading them.

— FSERP

fields.title: [Demo] Tip: end-of-shift checklist
fields.message:
Operations tip (sample)

Before closing a shift: reconcile cash and card batches, note any nozzle meter readings if your process requires it, and record tank dips when policy requires wet-stock verification.

Use Shifts and Tank Dips in the menu when your site enables those modules.

— FSERP

fields.title: [Demo] Feature highlight — printing from POS
fields.message:
Product update (sample)

From the POS screen you can print draft invoices, a short POS summary report, and customer A/R statements when a customer is selected — useful for counter copies and quick reconciliations.

— FSERP

fields.title: [Demo] Service renewal / licence expiry (sample)
fields.message:
Reminder (sample)

If your deployment includes time-bound licences or annual support, plan renewals before the expiry date so updates and helpdesk access stay active.

This message is only an example of how an expiry reminder could look.

— FSERP

Physical cash in registers and safes.

FSERP / system use: Default cash side for paid POS invoices and similar flows; also used for Payments received when 1020 is not in the chart.

Cash receipts not yet deposited to bank.

FSERP / system use: Undeposited funds — preferred debit for Payments received when no bank register is selected (both 1010 and 1020 present); batch deposits move from here into the bank GL.

Primary operating bank account.

FSERP / system use: Operating bank GL; payments received/made and bank-linked transfers when no per-bank GL is set. Owner cash contributions: debit 1030, credit 3000 (Owner Equity / Shareholder Capital) in a journal entry — link your Bank Account register to this chart account so the bank statement shows the same GL activity. Loans module: use as Settlement GL for disbursements/repayments when cash moves through this bank account.
Dedicated account for card acquirer settlements (optional).
Segregated statutory or tax trust account if required.

Credit sales to walk-in or commercial customers.

FSERP / system use: Trade receivables: credit invoices (sent/partial), customer payments received, and remaining AR on mark-paid.
Billed fleet cards and commercial charge accounts.

fields.account_name: Card Clearing — Visa / MC / Domestic Debit
fields.description:
Unsettled card batches (acquirer clearing).

FSERP / system use: Card / acquirer clearing: debit side for card POS sales when payment_method is card.
Secondary card networks with different settlement cycles.
Contra-AR reserve (credit balance; nets against receivables on the balance sheet).

Balance-sheet principal for funds you lent to others. Use as **Principal GL** on Loans → **Lent**.

FSERP / system use: Loans — principal receivable: Principal GL for money you **lent** (Loans → Lent). Chart type Loan, subtype loan receivable.

Tank inventory valued at cost (FIFO/weighted average per policy).

FSERP / system use: Fuel (wet-stock) inventory: credited when COGS posts for fuel lines with item cost.

Merchandise inventory for convenience retail.

FSERP / system use: Shop / c-store inventory: credited when COGS posts for non-fuel lines with item cost.
Car care, accessories, other resale goods.
Capital projects not yet placed in service.

Vendor invoices — general.

FSERP / system use: Trade payables: vendor bills (non-draft) and payment-made journals.

Collected sales or value-added tax remitted to authority.

FSERP / system use: Collected VAT / sales tax on invoices and bills (output and simplified input side on bills).
Fuel excise or carbon levies (rename per jurisdiction).
Employee or vendor withholding due to tax authority.
Net pay and accrued wages owed.
Social security, health, pension contributions payable.
Accrued utilities, interest, and other period costs.
Working capital facilities due within 12 months.

Balance-sheet principal for bank and third-party loans you owe. Use as **Principal GL** on Loans → **Borrowed**.

FSERP / system use: Loans — principal payable: Principal GL for money you **borrowed** (Loans → Borrowed). Chart type Loan, subtype loan payable.
Term loans and notes payable beyond 12 months.

Paid-in capital and owner contributions.

FSERP / system use: Owner Equity / Shareholder Capital (3000): credit this (and debit a bank/cash GL) via a manual journal when owners invest cash.
System opening balance offset during initial setup.

Distributions to owners (permanent accounts per policy).

FSERP / system use: Owner drawings / dividends: debit this (and credit bank/cash GL) when owners withdraw; use Journal Entries, not Fund Transfer.

Retail gasoline / petrol sales.

FSERP / system use: Revenue for fuel-grade / liter-based POS and invoice lines.
Fuel sold on credit to fleet accounts.

Merchandise and tobacco where permitted.

FSERP / system use: Revenue for shop / convenience-style POS and invoice lines.
Bottled lubes and additives sold at counter.
Wash bay and ancillary services revenue.

Air, vacuum, commissions, misc. operating.

FSERP / system use: Fallback revenue when a line does not map to 4100/4200.
Loyalty and pump discounts (contra-revenue; net against sales per policy).

Interest earned on lent funds. Optional **Interest GL** when splitting principal vs interest on **Lent** loans.

FSERP / system use: Loans — interest income on funds lent; optional Interest GL when splitting repayments on lent loans.

Fuel COGS (wet stock consumed) matched to fuel revenue.

FSERP / system use: COGS for fuel lines (debit) when items have unit cost.
Product cost for lube and fluids sold.

Merchandise COGS for shop.

FSERP / system use: COGS for shop lines (debit) when items have unit cost.
Tank loss, evaporation, meter variance beyond tolerance.
Water for wash and site use.
Pump repair, line maintenance, forecourt upkeep.
Gross wages before employer taxes and benefits.
Site, inventory, and business continuity coverage.
Card interchange, acquirer fees, bank service charges.

Interest paid on borrowed funds. Optional **Interest GL** when splitting principal vs interest on **Borrowed** loans.

FSERP / system use: Loans — interest expense on borrowings; optional Interest GL when splitting repayments on borrowed loans.

Supplies, postage, small tools not capitalized.

FSERP / system use: Default expense for vendor bills (full bill amount to expense when bill posts).

Charitable giving and local social support paid from station cash; use POS to debit expense and credit cash in hand (1010) or the active register.

FSERP / system use: Donation and social support paid from the station: record in POS; debits 6910 and credits 1010 Cash on Hand or the selected cash register’s linked GL (same as sales deposits).
Net loss on sale or retirement of assets.
Capitalize durable pond equipment, aerators, nets, and similar when following a fixed-asset policy.
Common credit side when posting pond profit transfers from management P&L into the books; pair with bank or cash.
Revenue from table-size / harvest fish sales (see Aquaculture income_type fish_harvest_sale).
Revenue from seed / fry sales (income_type fingerling_sale).
Processing, filleting, smoking, or other value-added services (income_type processing_value_add).

Soil cut and earthworks for pond construction or maintenance (soilcut).
Liming, fertilization, drying, and preparation before stocking (pond_preparation).

Miscellaneous pond costs (code other): boats, wiring, lighting, cameras, engines, aerators, nets, repairs, bikes, labour, site consumables, and items not mapped to a dedicated category.
